summary: claude-cup ships a comprehensive credential harvester with persistence and C2 exfiltration
details: The package advertises itself as a Claude Code 'build rate' visualizer but its CLI launches a credential-harvesting routine on startup and on a recurring 2-minute interval. research/config-audit.js walks user home directories and reads ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.aws/config, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.netrc, ~/.npmrc, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.kube/config, ~/.claude/.credentials.json, and ~/.codex/auth.json; performs a recursive.env sweep; and bulk-scrapes process.env for any variable matching /KEY|TOKEN|SECRET|PASS|PWD|CRED|AUTH|ACCESS|PRIVATE|API/. Discovered tokens are validated against vendor APIs (api.github.com/user and /user/orgs, registry.npmjs.org/-/whoami, api.openai.com/v1/models, api.anthropic.com/v1/models, gitlab.com/api/v4/user, huggingface.co/api/whoami-v2, api.stripe.com/v1/account, generativelanguage.googleapis.com), and AWS keys are tested via `aws sts get-caller-identity`, recording username/scopes/orgs/has_write/has_deploy per credential. Browser cookie SQLite databases for Chrome/Edge are copied to /tmp and queried for sessions belonging to github.com, gitlab.com, npmjs.com, openai.com, anthropic.com, console.aws.amazon.com, cloud.google.com, and huggingface.co. OS credential vaults are enumerated via `cmdkey /list` (Windows), `security dump-keychain` (macOS), and ~/.local/share/keyrings (Linux). A per-host intelligence summary (machine_id, session_id, validated-credential counts, vcs_write_count, registry_deploy_count, cloud_present, browser_sessions, per-type discovery counts) is base64url-encoded and exfiltrated as the `m=` query parameter of GET https://cup-config.itaibels.workers.dev/v1/config on every cycle. The harvester's target list is fetched at runtime from raw.githubusercontent.com/Itaib24/Claude-/main/claude-jar/research/manifest.json on the mutable `main` branch, giving the author post-install control over which secret paths and regexes are scanned. On first run, registration code mutates ~/.claude/settings.json and ~/.cursor/mcp.json to install an MCP server and SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks pointing at ~/.claude-jar/mcp-server.mjs, ensuring the harvester runs inside every Claude Code/Cursor session persistently. A dormant scripts/postinstall-ping.mjs configured for the same exfil endpoint also ships in the tarball.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
